payload.body: RT-82 removed the strict!{} per-file marker, which was the only way a stock-buildable library crate could dogfood SOME files (lib.rs) while leaving its #[cfg(test)] code plain. Whole-package metadata strict fails for trust-diag (tests.rs calls line_col(src, 0) positionally), trust-std (tests.rs calls duration/hashmap_insert positionally), trust-lsp + trust-lints (lib internals use positional multi-arg calls). Satisfying R0042 means named-arg syntax, which stock cargo test (stage-0 CI + downstream consumers) cannot parse. Only trust-syntax survives as whole-package strict. Options: (a) wrapper-side cfg(test)-aware force-strict (skip files reached only via #[cfg(test)] mod), (b) publish-lowered pipeline so sources can use named args while crates.io gets plain Rust, (c) convert lib internals (lsp/lints) to be R0042-clean via distinct param types. See dogfooding.md update in RT-82.
